# Palm Oil Producer / Trader Site — Nigeria-First Research Brief

**Niche ID:** P3-palm-oil-trader-palm-oil-trader-site  
**Vertical slug:** palm-oil-trader  
**Niche family:** NF-AGR-COM (anchor)  
**Family role:** anchor  
**Research date:** 2026-04-26

## 1. Nigerian Business Reality
Nigeria is Africa's third-largest palm oil producer (after Indonesia and Malaysia), producing approximately 1.2 million metric tonnes annually. Edo, Delta, Imo, Rivers, Cross River, Ogun, and Ondo states are the primary production zones. The market is split between crude palm oil (CPO) and refined palm olein, palm kernel oil (PKO), and kernel cake. Local retail price: ₦3,500–₦6,500 per litre; wholesale drum (25 litres): ₦60,000–₦150,000; industrial drums (200 litres): ₦350,000–₦700,000. The sector is dominated by smallholder farmers and medium traders who aggregate from local mills. Export requires NAFDAC and NADP certification. Domestic B2B buyers include food processors, soap manufacturers, and hotels.

## 2. Target Audience Profile
**Persona A — Mr Chinedu Obi, 42, Owerri**: Palm oil aggregator sourcing from Imo State mills. Wants to attract bulk buyers (food processors, Dangote Flour) online rather than waiting at Owerri market.
**Persona B — Mrs Biodun Adesanya, 38, Lagos (Mushin)**: Restaurant owner buying 25-litre drums monthly. Wants reliable supplier with consistent quality and same-day delivery within Lagos.
**Persona C — Emeka Eze, 29, Enugu**: Young agripreneur wanting to build a palm oil export brand. Needs a professional website to approach EU/UK buyers via NADP certification.

## 3. Recommended Website Structure
**Home:** Hero (brand name, "Pure. Traceable. Nigerian." tagline, WhatsApp order CTA), product grid (CPO, PKO, kernel cake — price per litre/drum in ₦), origin story (Edo/Delta/Imo mills), NAFDAC badge, bulk pricing CTA.
**About:** Farm/aggregation story, production process, quality standards, sourcing states.
**Products:** Detailed price list (retail/wholesale/export), drum sizes, delivery terms, minimum orders.
**Contact:** WhatsApp ordering, address, export enquiry form with NDPR.

## 4. Content Tone & Voice
Earthy, genuine, pride-of-origin. "Straight from the Nigerian palm belt." Primary CTA: "Order via WhatsApp" (bulk) / "Request Export Quote." AVOID generic stock photos of refined oil; show Nigerian palm fruit, local extraction mills.

## 5. Trust Signal Inventory
NAFDAC registration number; NADP (Nigerian Agricultural Development Platform) export certification if applicable; FMARD compliance; origin state badge ("Sourced from Edo & Delta mills"); years in business; processing capacity ("5,000 litres/day"); client testimonials (food processors, hotels); CAC registration; payment via bank transfer, Paystack, POS.

## 6. Image Direction
1. Nigerian palm fruit bunch — bright orange-red, harvested from plantation, Edo State setting.
2. Traditional palm oil extraction — local press, raw CPO flowing into containers, rural mill.
3. Drums of palm oil ready for distribution — branded 25-litre yellow jerricans stacked in warehouse.
4. Quality check — oil poured into glass container showing clear orange-red colour.
5. Loading truck at Owerri or Benin City aggregation centre for Lagos delivery.
6. Export packaging — sealed containers with NAFDAC stickers for international markets.
7. Farmer in plantation walking through palm trees — origin, traceability, sustainability.

## 7. Regulatory Compliance Checklist
NAFDAC: processing and export of food oil requires NAFDAC registration number. NADP/NEXIM: export certification required for international sales. FMARD: food processing compliance. SON: quality standards for domestic retail. CAC: business registration. NDPR for contact forms.

## 8. Nigeria-First Compliance Checklist
| Requirement | Status |
|---|---|
| WhatsApp CTA home + contact | ✅ REQUIRED |
| All prices in ₦ (per litre/drum) | ✅ REQUIRED |
| Nigerian states of origin | ✅ Edo, Delta, Imo, Rivers |
| NAFDAC trust signal | ✅ REQUIRED |
| Paystack / bank transfer | ✅ REQUIRED |
| Mobile-first 375px | ✅ REQUIRED |
| NDPR consent | ✅ REQUIRED |
